dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8830 IH: Flood Mapping Modernization and Homeowner Empowerment Pilot Program Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Quigley introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure, and in addition to the Committee on Financial Services, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned

form.official-title: To require the Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency to carry out a pilot program to enhance the mapping of urban flooding and associated property damage and the availability of that mapped data to homeowners, businesses, and localities to help understand and mitigate the risk of such flooding, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Flood Mapping Modernization and Homeowner Empowerment Pilot Program Act of 2026.

section.header: Flood mapping modernization and homeowner empowerment pilot program

paragraph.text: The term Administrator means the Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency.
The term coastal means, with respect to a unit of general local government, that the unit borders a body of water that—

subparagraph.text: is more than 2,000 square miles in size; and
is not a river.

paragraph.text: The term pelagic means, with respect to a unit of general local government, that—

subparagraph.text: the unit is a coastal unit; and
the body of water that the unit borders is—

clause.text: a large, open body of water, including a bay or a gulf, that empties into an ocean.

paragraph.text: The term pilot program means the pilot program carried out by the Administrator under this section.
The term urban flooding—

subparagraph.text: means the inundation, by water, of property in a built environment, particularly in a densely populated area, that—

subclause.text: collecting on an impervious surface; or
increasing the level of a body of water that is located near that built environment; and

clause.text: overwhelms the capacity of drainage systems in the built environment, such as storm sewers;

subparagraph.text: includes—

clause.text: a situation in which stormwater enters a building through a window, door, or other opening;
the backup of water through a sewer pipe, shower, toilet, sink, or floor drain;
the seepage of water through a wall or a floor;
the accumulation of water on property or a public right-of-way; and
the overflow from a body of water, such as a river, lake, or ocean; and

subparagraph.text: does not include flooding in an undeveloped or agricultural area.

paragraph.text: The term urbanized area means an area that has been defined and designated as an urbanized area by the Bureau of the Census during the most recently completed decennial census.

subsection.text: The Administrator shall carry out a pilot program to make grants to units of local government to—

paragraph.text: enhance the production of maps relating to urban flooding and associated property damage; and
increase the availability of the maps described in paragraph (1) to homeowners, businesses, and units of local government to enable those entities to minimize the risk of urban flooding.

subsection.text: Amounts from grants made under the pilot program may be used only to carry out activities that meet the following objectives:

paragraph.text: Developing a methodology for assessing the risk of urban flooding through the deployment of technology-based mapping tools that—

subparagraph.text: are easily understandable by the public; and
effectively convey information regarding the level of flood risk.

paragraph.text: Providing structure-specific projections of annual chance flood frequency.
Providing structure-based flood risk assessments.
Providing program design for the mitigation of the risk of urban flooding.
Incorporating information regarding climate trends into urban flooding risk assessments.
Making the information described in this subsection publicly available on the internet through a web-based portal so as to increase transparency regarding homeowner flood risks.
A grant under the pilot program may be made only to—

subparagraph.text: a unit of general local government that is located in an urbanized area with a population of more than 50,000 individuals; or
a stormwater management authority of a unit of general local government described in subparagraph (A).

paragraph.text: A grant under the pilot program may not be made to—

subparagraph.text: any unit of general local government, or the stormwater management authority of a unit of general local government, that previously received a grant under the pilot program;
any unit of general local government if the stormwater management agency for that unit previously received a grant under the pilot program; or
any stormwater management agency of a unit of general local government if that unit previously received a grant under the pilot program.

paragraph.header: Treatment of certain stormwater management authorities

subparagraph.text: In the case of a stormwater management authority that operates with respect to more than 1 unit of general local government, the application of that authority shall be considered for purposes of paragraph (2) of this subsection and subsections (f), (g), and (h)(1) to be made for the largest unit of general local government with respect to which that authority operates.

Nothing in subparagraph (A) may be construed to limit the ability of a stormwater management authority described in that subparagraph to carry out activities under a demonstration project in any other jurisdiction in, or with respect to any other unit of general local government with, which that authority operates.

subsection.text: To be eligible for a grant under the pilot program, a unit of general local government or a stormwater management agency shall submit to the Administrator an application in such form and containing such information as the Administrator shall require.

paragraph.text: Subject to paragraph (2), and to the submission of approvable applications, in each fiscal year for which amounts are made available for grants under the pilot program, the Administrator shall select, from among applications submitted under subsection (e) for that fiscal year, 3 units of general local government or stormwater management authorities to receive grants under the pilot program.

Subject only to the submission of approvable applications, the Administrator shall select, in the aggregate over the entire duration of the pilot program, 12 units of general local government or stormwater management authorities to receive grants under the pilot program, as follows:

subparagraph.text: Three of the applicants selected shall be units of general local government, or stormwater management authorities for those units, each of which has a population of more than 800,000 individuals, as follows:

subclause.text: a unit of general local government that is a pelagic unit; or
a stormwater authority for a unit described in subclause (I).
a unit of general local government that—

item.text: is not a pelagic unit; or

subclause.text: a stormwater authority for a unit described in subclause (I).
a unit of general local government that is not a coastal unit; or
a stormwater authority for a unit described in subclause (I).

subparagraph.text: Six of the applicants selected shall be units of general local government, or stormwater management authorities for those units, each of which has a population of more than 200,000 individuals and not more than 800,000 individuals, as follows:

subclause.text: units of general local government that are coastal units; or
stormwater management authorities for units described in subclause (I).
units of general local government that are not coastal units; or
stormwater management authorities for units described in subclause (I).

subparagraph.text: Three of the applicants selected shall be—

clause.text: units of general local government, each of which has a population of more than 50,000 individuals and not more than 200,000 individuals; or
stormwater management authorities for units described in clause (i).

paragraph.text: The Administrator shall select applicants for grants under the pilot program based on the extent to which the applications of those applicants shall achieve the objectives described in subsection (c).
In selecting applicants to receive grants under the pilot program under subparagraphs (B) and (C) of subsection (f)(2), the Administrator shall give priority to applicants—

subparagraph.text: that are highly vulnerable to sea level rise;
within which are located a military installation or another facility relating to national security concerns; or
that have—

clause.text: populations that are highly vulnerable to urban flooding; and
an uneven capacity for flood mitigation and response efforts resulting from socioeconomic factors.

paragraph.text: In determining the amount of a grant under the pilot program, the Administrator shall consider the population of the grant recipient, which may be considered in terms of the tier under subsection (f)(2) with respect to the recipient.
The amount of a grant under the pilot program may not exceed 75 percent of the total cost incurred in carrying out the activities described in subsection (c).

subsection.text: The Administrator shall require each recipient of a grant under the pilot program to complete the activities described in subsection (c), which shall be, subject to subsection (h)(2), carried out using the grant amounts, not later than 18 months after the date on which the recipient initially receives the grant amounts under the pilot program.
The Administrator shall make all determinations regarding population under the pilot program by using data from the most recently completed decennial census by the Bureau of the Census.

Each recipient of a grant under the pilot program shall, not later than 30 months after the date on which the recipient initially receives the grant amounts, submit to the Administrator a report that describes—

paragraph.text: the activities carried out with the grant amounts;
how the activities carried out with the grant amounts have met the objectives described in subsection (c);
any lessons learned in carrying out the activities described in paragraph (2); and
any recommendations for future mapping modernization efforts by the Federal Emergency Management Agency.

subsection.text: Not later than 2 years after the date of enactment of this Act, and not less frequently than once every 2 years thereafter until the date on which all activities carried out with amounts from grants under the pilot program are completed, the Administrator shall submit to Congress and make available to the public on an internet website a report that—

paragraph.text: describes—

subparagraph.text: the progress of the activities carried out with amounts from those grants; and
the effectiveness of technology-based mapping tools used in carrying out the activities described in subparagraph (A); and

paragraph.text: with respect to the final report that the Administrator is required to submit under this subsection, includes recommendations to Congress and the executive branch of the Federal Government for implementing strategies, practices, and technologies to mitigate the effects of urban flooding.

subsection.text: It is the sense of Congress that, because the pilot program is limited with respect to scope and resources, communities that participate in the pilot program should acknowledge that the most successful efforts to mitigate the effects of urban flooding—

paragraph.text: take a structural-based mitigation approach with respect to construction, which includes—

subparagraph.text: recognizing any post-storm damage that may occur; and
pursuing designs that proactively minimize future flood damage;

paragraph.text: make individuals in the community aware, through any cost-effective and available means of education, of the best approaches regarding the construction of properties that are able to survive floods, which reduces the cost of future repairs; and

encourage home and property owners to consider the measures described in paragraphs (1) and (2), which are the most cost-effective and prudent ways to reduce the impact of flooding, when constructing or renovating building components.

subsection.text: There are authorized to be appropriated for grants under the pilot program—

paragraph.text: $2,000,000 for fiscal year 2027; and
$5,000,000 for fiscal year 2028, to remain available through fiscal year 2030.
